title: PubNub publish keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PubNub Publish Key detected. PubNub is a real-time messaging and data streaming platform. Publish keys allow publishing messages to channels, broadcasting data to connected clients, and triggering events. Exposing publish keys enables attackers to send unauthorized messages, spam channels, exhaust your message quota, impersonate legitimate publishers, and potentially execute denial-of-service attacks on your real-time infrastructure. Revoke this key immediately from your PubNub Admin Portal and generate a new one. Store keys securely in environment variables or secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault, etc.).

PubNub Publish Key detected in variable assignment. Publish keys provide write access to PubNub channels, allowing message publishing, presence updates, and channel management. Exposing these credentials enables attackers to send malicious messages, flood channels with spam, impersonate legitimate users, disrupt real-time communication, and exhaust your account quota. Immediately revoke the exposed key and generate a new one. Implement secure key storage using environment variables or dedicated secret management services.
